component.name: Franz
component.summary: All your messaging in one app

description.p: Franz brings WhatsApp, Slack, Telegram, Signal, Gmail, Outlook and many more messaging and email services together in a single desktop app, so you can keep every conversation in one place instead of juggling browser tabs and native apps.

ul.li: Combine dozens of chat and email services in one window
Organize services into workspaces for work, personal and side projects
AI-powered summaries and a privacy-conscious assistant with local model support
Native Signal integration and WhatsApp support
Notifications, unread badges and quiet hours across every service

screenshot.caption: The unified inbox — emails from every account in one place
The Franz Assistant — AI summaries with a privacy-conscious assistant

ul.li: Full right-to-left layout for Arabic, Hebrew, Farsi, and Urdu — sidebar, services, settings, popups, popped-out windows and previews all mirror to match the system text direction. The search field, email composer, and assistant align text correctly regardless of the app's display language.
Unread badges in the horizontal tab bar are smaller and tidier.
Email drafts no longer lose their attachments when reopened.
Multi-paragraph emails are no longer squashed together in the composer.
A startup crash on some Windows machines is fixed.
French translation: the entire app — menus, settings, the email view, everything — is now available in French.
A new About page in Settings with an optional Beta program that follows your account across devices.
Link preview on hover, with ⌘/Ctrl/Shift+click to open the opposite of your usual setting.
The workspace switcher and Privacy Shield now slide down inside the window instead of opening as separate popups.
Dark mode preference now persists across restarts.
Email sync no longer gets stuck.
Signal on Windows can complete its initial link.
Franz no longer quits unexpectedly on Windows when syncing email attachments in the background. PDF text extraction now runs isolated from the main process.
Windows auto-update installs and relaunches reliably again.
Window controls on extra windows (external links, email preview, popped-out services) are visible and correctly bound on Windows and Linux.

Ghosts — a dedicated view in Franz Mail that surfaces relationships cooling off (Cooling, Cold, Frozen). All computed on-device. Free plan shows the three warmest quiet relationships; Franz Pro opens the full list.
Bring-your-own Google Gemini key for the Pro AI assistant.
Cmd+K searches inside WhatsApp messages, not just chat names.
Overdue todos count toward the dock badge.
Draft handling, smoother reply sending, and pop-up window behaviour on Windows and Linux.

Franz 6 ships natively on Linux as AppImage, .deb, and snap on x64 and arm64. Native window decorations, system tray (KDE; GNOME via AppIndicator), autostart toggle that writes a proper XDG entry, live OS theme sync, and franz:// + mailto: handlers register the same way they do on macOS and Windows. Signal device-linking works on Linux arm64.
Mute all services button at the bottom of the sidebar; each service tab gets its own mute icon.
Email search reads the message body, not just the subject.
Frozen email folders, smart-reply line breaks, WhatsApp typing lag.

Structured catch-me-up briefings in Franz Assistant: emails grouped by Urgent / Important / Informational with summary cards and inline Reply/Snooze/Archive/Remind; todos grouped by Overdue / Due today / Upcoming with inline checkboxes.
Settings → Network toggle for managed corporate networks where Gmail or WhatsApp wouldn't load.
Inline images display inside HTML emails.
Help → "What's new in this version" opens an in-app summary.
Gmail sign-in works again for consumer accounts.
Corporate authenticated-proxy networks no longer swallow Franz's outbound requests.
Mattermost and Rocket.Chat regain the Hosted / Self-hosted choice.
Signal opens cleanly on a fresh install.
Email accounts (Gmail / Outlook) sync across the devices you sign in on. Synced accounts arrive with a one-click Reconnect button.
Unverified-email banner with a Resend confirmation button; the Franz Assistant stays gated until the email is confirmed.
Cross-machine setup no longer leaves the app in a half-signed-in state — an unreadable session asks you to sign in again.
Sign-in on Windows after a cold start no longer hangs at the Cloudflare challenge.
Window controls on Windows: the new app-menu button stops overlapping the page-header icons.
Newly arrived emails in the global inbox flip from bold to read the moment you open them.

description.p: Franz 6 — the version that marks ten years of Franz. A from-the-ground-up rebuild combining messaging, mail, and AI into one window, designed around what a decade of stubborn iteration has taught us about how people actually use messengers.
